CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Set For Trial On Oct-15-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Plaintiffs Christopher Dupuy, Rockefeller Capital Management, L.P., Rockefeller Financial Llc, And Rockefeller & Co. Llc'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
A. Marisa Chun, Esq. 650-815-7668, Pankit Doshi, Esq. 650-815-7470, Evan Belosa, Esq. 212-547-5869 of McDermott Will & Emery for Plaintiff.
Stephen Tedesco, Esq. 415-677-3134 of Littler for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues and Plaintiff seeks clarification on the last sentence of the tentative ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiffs Christopher Dupuy, Rockefeller Capital Management, L.P., Rockefeller Financial Llc, And Rockefeller & Co. Llc'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs Rockefeller Capital Management L.P., Rockefeller & Co. LLC, Rockefeller Financial LLC, and Christopher Dupuy's motion for preliminary injunction is granted in substantial part. Until there is a final determination on plaintiffs' request for injunctive relief or an order of this court to the contrary, defendant Focus Financial Partners LLC and its officers, agents, employees, representatives, and all persons acting in concert or participating with them are restrained and enjoined from directly or indirectly seeking to enforce its non-competition and non-solicitation restrictive covenants as to Mr. Dupuy in California. This order does not preclude Focus from continuing to litigate in Delaware nor does it preclude Focus from seeking to protect what it asserts as trade secrets or other protected information under the California Uniform Trade Secrets Act, the Defend Trade Secrets Act, or other laws. Mr. Dupuy may work for the Rockefeller entities in California provided that in doing so Mr. Dupuy and the Rockefeller entities are not in violation of the August 28, 2018 order from the Delaware Chancery Court prohibiting the use of Focus' Confidential Information. The sole forum selection clause in the Dupuy/Focus agreements is in the November 22, 2017 agreement and that clause is invalid per Labor Code 925 based on the record which supports plaintiffs' position that Mr. Dupuy's receipt of units per that agreement was compensation for his employment. Although there is no case on point, this court interprets the phrase "condition of employment" in section 925 as including any requirement to receive employment compensation. The choice of law provisions in the Dupuy/Focus agreements are invalid per Nedlloyd Lines B.V. v. Superior Court (1992) 3 Cal. 4th 459 because there is a conflict between California and Delaware law regarding the enforceability of the restrictive covenants and California has a materially greater interest than Delaware regarding the enforceability of the restrictive covenants. As an alternative holding, the sole forum selection clause and the choice of law provisions are invalid per Verdugo v. Alliantgroup, L.P. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 141 because Business and Professions Code 16600 is an unwaivable right for a California employee and Focus contests the application of that statute in Delaware. Plaintiffs have demonstrated a likelihood of prevailing on the merits that the restrictive covenants are unenforceable per section 16600. (see word minutes for full details)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
